result.learned: BENE's meta-harness (bene-main, implementing "Meta-Harness" arXiv:2603.28052 + CORAL arXiv:2604.01658 per its docs) is a resumable, fully-recorded evolutionary search over harness CODE, backed by a single SQLite bene.db. (1) SEARCH MODEL: a candidate is a HarnessCandidate — ULID id + complete Python source defining `run(problem)->dict` + parent_ids lineage + iteration + rationale metadata; two-stage validation (AST check for run(), then import/exec smoke test). An iteration = proposer reads a compaction-leveled archive digest (levels 0-10, default 5; error-pattern extraction beats raw dumps per their measurements) → submits k candidates (default 2) → HarnessEvaluator executes each candidate's run() IN-PROCESS per problem (with an injected `llm()` callable backed by TierRouter), scores via the Benchmark, stores per-problem traces/scores in the search agent's VFS archive (/harnesses/<id>/{source.py,scores.json,trace.jsonl,per_problem.jsonl,metadata.json,diagnosis.json}) → recomputes the frontier. The frontier IS already multi-objective Pareto: pareto.py implements strict dominance over named maximize/minimize objectives (default ["+accuracy","-context_cost"]; benchmarks define their own — arc_agi3 uses +rhae/-actions), persisted as /pareto/frontier.json. CORAL layers: stagnation detection → forced PIVOT digest banner; /attempts /notes /skills memory; a persistent "bene-knowledge" agent carries winning harnesses+skills forward as seeds for future searches (max_prior_seeds=5). (2) GATING: kernel/eval Probes are pre-registered, canonicalized, sha256-locked (edit → LockTamperError, no retune-and-rerun), admissibility-self-tested (a gate that can't kill the baseline → VOID); verdicts ACCEPT/REJECT/VOID are engrams; heldout.py adds sha256 train/heldout manifest hashing with overlap⇒VOID anti-overfit. promote() requires an ACCEPT verdict linked to the candidate (PromotionBlocked otherwise). The mh→gate loop IS now wired: every candidate evaluated on the autonomous path is bridged to a tier-4 strategic genome engram (_bridge_candidate_to_evolve, always), and opt-in SearchConfig.auto_promote (default False) runs auto_promote_evolved — an automated improvement-probe + gated_promote that does NOT weaken the gate. GAP: the collaborative MCP path (mh_next_iteration) writes archive files directly and never bridges or auto-promotes. Autonomy ladder L0-L4 is enforced in code with per-agent per-domain grants; L4 (autonomous-promote) requires granted_by="human:<name>" (hard ValueError) and is excluded from trust-driven auto_promotion (hard-capped at L3); every denial emits a trust engram. (3) COEVOLUTION: mh_spawn_coevolution(benchmark, n_agents 2-4) spawns N independent search agents + one hub agent; each is driven independently via mh_submit_candidate + mh_next_iteration; hub sync auto-fires every hub_sync_interval (default 2) iterations inside mh_next_iteration (or manually via mh_hub_sync), pushing each agent's top-3 frontier harnesses + skills to the hub and pulling peers' best into the local archive where they enter the local Pareto frontier. (4) EXTERNAL EXECUTORS — three seams: (a) PROPOSER seam: collaborative session mode (mh_start_search → digest; mh_submit_candidate; mh_next_iteration) makes the CALLING MCP agent the proposer — the tool description literally targets Claude Code ("inference happens in YOUR session, zero extra cost"); any process that can call MCP/CLI can submit candidates. (b) MODEL seam: TierRouter providers include claude_code (ClaudeCodeProvider shells `claude --print` on the user's Claude subscription — exactly BYO-creds) and agent_sdk (claude-agent-sdk subprocess), so a Claude Code invocation can sit INSIDE an mh evaluation via the injected llm(). (c) BENCHMARK seam: Benchmark ABC (objectives, get_search_set/get_test_set, score, seeds, diagnostic_view) + register_benchmark registry; arc_agi3.py proves a full external environment (ARC-AGI-3 arcengine SDK, RHAE scoring) works as an mh benchmark — but the MCP server and background worker HARDCODE the benchmark import list, so new ladders need bene code changes or the Python API. Weco appears NOWHERE in bene-main — zero existing integration; that seam must be built. (5) STORAGE: everything (VFS files, KV state, tool calls, event journal, checkpoints, append-only typed engrams tiers 0-4 with mandatory provenance and >4KB blobs content-addressed, probe registry, autonomy grants, trust, shared log) lives in ONE WAL-mode SQLite file; `cp bene.db` is a full backup; mh_resume rebuilds search state from the archive and continues under the original config; `--background` runs a detached worker process that survives parent exit, polled via `bene mh status`. Honesty flags: SKILL.md (2026-06-16) still says the mh→kill-gate loop is "NOT wired end-to-end" citing docs/design/CLAIMS-AUDIT.md, which no longer exists in the tree (a branch fix/landing-delink-claims-audit exists) — the working-tree code supersedes that claim but default behavior (auto_promote=False) still requires explicit opt-in; evaluator exec() is logical isolation only (SKILL.md: use containers for untrusted code); per-problem eval timeout is timeout_seconds/len(problems) (default 60s total), too tight for long agentic ladder runs without config.

evidence.note: Search model walkthrough: archive layout, proposer tools (mh_ls/read/grep_archive, mh_submit_harness), two-stage validation, digest compaction levels + measured quality table, CORAL pivot/memory/coevolution, resume semantics, text-only-proposer fallback (```python block extraction), paper citations arXiv:2603.28052 + 2604.01658
Multi-objective Pareto: strict dominance over {name: maximize|minimize}, compute_pareto keeps non-dominated points only — frontier is genuinely multi-objective, not scalarized

HarnessCandidate (ULID, source_code, parent_ids, iteration, metadata) + validate_interface (AST + import smoke test); SearchConfig incl. objectives list ('+acc','-cost' syntax), auto_promote/auto_promote_delta, stagnation_threshold, harness_timeout_seconds=60 divided per problem

Full mh_* MCP surface: mh_start_search seeds+digest ('YOU (Claude Code)... inference happens in YOUR session'); mh_submit_candidate validates + queues pending; mh_next_iteration evaluates pending, recomputes frontier from ALL /harnesses/*/scores.json (incl. hub-pulled), CORAL stagnation/pivot state, auto hub-sync every interval; mh_spawn_coevolution creates N archives + hub agent; _do_hub_sync pushes top-3 frontier + skills, pulls peers' best; hardcoded _import_benchmarks list; NOTE collaborative path never calls _store_result → no genome bridge/auto-promote

_store_result always calls _bridge_candidate_to_evolve (tier-4 genome engram, 'deliberately stops short of promotion... promote() still requires an ACCEPT verdict') and _maybe_auto_promote when config.auto_promote; improved/failed results persisted to cross-agent MemoryStore; 'bene-knowledge' persistent agent

auto_promote_evolved = build_improvement_probe (relative-to-baseline gate, identity self-test makes it admissible) + gated_promote (promote iff ACCEPT verdict linked verifies→candidate) — 'automated ... WITHOUT weakening the kill gate ... no back-door auto-flip'

Probes pre-registered, gate spec canonicalized + sha256-locked (LockTamperError on edit), admissibility checked at registration (can't-kill-baseline → inadmissible → VOID); heldout manifest sha256 hashing, train/heldout overlap ⇒ VOID

Autonomy ladder L0 observe → L4 autonomous-promote enforced in code; per-agent per-domain grants; L4 requires granted_by='human:<name>' else ValueError; trust-driven auto_promote hard-capped at L3; denials emit trust engrams

Evaluation spawns a BENE agent per candidate, execs harness source in-process, injects llm(prompt) callable backed by TierRouter; per-problem timeout = timeout_seconds/len(problems); Surrogate Verifier diagnosis attached; rich traces ('paper ablation: raw traces beat scores-only by 15+ points')

ClaudeCodeProvider shells `claude --print` (user's Claude subscription, BYO creds); agent_sdk provider uses claude-agent-sdk SubprocessCLITransport — a Claude Code invocation can be the model inside an mh evaluation

ARC-AGI-3 already ships as an mh benchmark: runs agent code against local arcengine, RHAE scoring, objectives maximize-RHAE/minimize-actions — existence proof that an external ladder environment can be an mh Benchmark; Benchmark ABC = the ladder-adapter interface; registry exists but server/worker imports are hardcoded

Single WAL-mode SQLite bene.db holds all durable state ('cp bene.db backup.db'); engrams = append-only typed log, tiers 0-4, mandatory provenance, >4KB → content-addressed blobs; detached background worker survives parent exit, resumable via --search-agent-id

evidence.cite: grep -rni weco /home/admin/gh/bene-main/{bene,docs} → 0 hits; /home/admin/.claude/skills/bene/SKILL.md (lines 229, 283-285); find CLAIMS-AUDIT → only git branch fix/landing-delink-claims-audit
evidence.note: No weco integration exists anywhere in bene-main; SKILL.md's 'mh→kill-gate NOT wired / CLAIMS-AUDIT: partial' claim is stale vs working-tree code (autopromote.py exists, opt-in) and its cited CLAIMS-AUDIT.md file is gone

Prior art: ADR-0014 already chose meta-harness evolution over RL, with promotion hard-gated on a BENE ACCEPT verdict and finish_success gated on the BENE verdict — the redesign's gating contract is a generalization of this

next: Weco lane: verify whether the weco CLI can be scripted/wrapped to act as an mh proposer (submit its best solution via mh_submit_candidate) or whether it must own its own loop — bene-main gives zero guidance (no integration exists)

Design synthesis: pick ONE resolution for the proposer-role overlap between Weco and mh (Weco-inside-mh session mode / mh-as-outer-ledger-over-weco-runs / reuse only pareto.py + eval gating as libraries) and record it in DESIGN.md + ADR-0015

Prototype spike: a custom Benchmark subclass whose run() shells out to a user's Claude Code agent (pattern proven by arc_agi3 + ClaudeCodeProvider) to validate that a whole-agent evaluation fits the mh evaluation contract, incl. raising harness_timeout_seconds for long agentic runs

If collaborative session mode is chosen as the integration, file/fix the mh_next_iteration gap (no _bridge_candidate_to_evolve / auto-promote on that path) upstream in bene-main, or route promotion through the manual R3 genome_from_candidate bridge in agentdex
Reconcile SKILL.md staleness (CLAIMS-AUDIT.md delinked, autopromote now wired opt-in) with the bene maintainer before citing either claim in ADR-0015
Verify the two paper claims load-bearing for marketing copy (6x harness spread, 3-10x coevolution) against arXiv:2603.28052 / 2604.01658 directly (web lane) — currently sourced only from bene docs
key_facts: A meta-harness candidate is a complete Python program defining run(problem)->dict, with ULID id, parent_ids lineage, and rationale; validated by AST check + import smoke test before any evaluation (bene/metaharness/harness.py)

The frontier is already multi-objective Pareto: strict-dominance filtering over named maximize/minimize objectives, persisted as /pareto/frontier.json in the search agent's VFS (bene/metaharness/pareto.py)

Two drive modes exist: autonomous (mh_search / bene mh search, incl. detached --background worker) where bene runs its own proposer LLM, and collaborative session mode (mh_start_search → mh_submit_candidate → mh_next_iteration) where the calling MCP agent (designed for Claude Code) is the proposer at zero extra inference cost

Kill-gate contract: probes are pre-registered with sha256-locked gate specs (tamper → LockTamperError), admissibility-self-tested (can't-kill-baseline → VOID), verdicts are engrams, and promote() refuses without an ACCEPT verdict linked to the candidate (PromotionBlocked)

The mh→kill-gate loop is wired on the autonomous path: every evaluated candidate becomes a tier-4 genome engram (_bridge_candidate_to_evolve, unconditional) and opt-in SearchConfig.auto_promote (default False) runs a gated improvement probe via auto_promote_evolved — the collaborative MCP path does NOT bridge or auto-promote

Autonomy ladder L0-L4 is code-enforced per-agent per-domain; L4 (autonomous-promote) requires granted_by='human:<name>' and is excluded from trust-driven auto-promotion (hard cap L3); denials emit trust engrams

mh_spawn_coevolution creates N independent search archives + one hub agent; hub sync (auto every 2 iterations inside mh_next_iteration) pushes top-3 frontier harnesses + skills and pulls peers' best, which enter the local Pareto frontier

Evaluated harnesses receive an injected llm() callable routed through TierRouter, whose providers include claude_code (`claude --print` on the user's subscription) and agent_sdk (claude-agent-sdk) — BYO-creds Claude Code execution inside an mh iteration works today

An ARC-AGI-3 benchmark adapter already ships in bene (bene/metaharness/benchmarks/arc_agi3.py): runs candidate agent code against the local arcengine SDK, scores RHAE, objectives +rhae/-actions — one of agentdex's six target ladders

Benchmark ABC (objectives, search/test split, score, seeds, diagnostic_view) + register_benchmark is the extension point, but the MCP server and worker hardcode the benchmark import list — new ladders require bene code changes or the Python API

All state — archive, traces, engrams, probes, verdicts, checkpoints, autonomy grants — lives in one WAL-mode SQLite bene.db; cp is a full backup; mh_resume rebuilds and continues an interrupted search; a 'bene-knowledge' agent carries winning harnesses/skills into future searches as seeds
Weco appears nowhere in bene-main (zero grep hits) — no existing weco↔bene integration

Evaluator executes candidate source in-process via exec() (logical isolation only; SKILL.md: use containers for untrusted code) and divides the 60s default timeout across all problems — both are gaps for long/untrusted whole-agent ladder runs
design_implications: Division of labor — meta-harness owns the OUTER loop: candidate lineage (parent_ids + append-only archive + tier-4 genome engrams), the multi-objective Pareto frontier, hash-locked promotion gating, coevolution population, cross-search knowledge, and crash-resumability. This is exactly the 'measure + gate' spine the agentdex engine needs and should be reused, not rebuilt.

Weco owns the INNER loop (metric-driven code mutation against an eval command) — but this DIRECTLY OVERLAPS mh's proposer role: both propose code changes and keep what scores higher. The design must resolve this explicitly; the cleanest split given the code: Weco = proposer (mutation engine), mh = evaluator + frontier + gate + ledger, wired through the collaborative session-mode tools (mh_submit_candidate / mh_next_iteration), which were built precisely so an external agent supplies candidates. Alternative: treat each whole weco run as one mh iteration and submit only its best solution. Running both loops un-reconciled = two competing self-improvement loops (honest conflict).

agentdex owns the measurement CONTRACT + ledger + leaderboard: ladder-adapter definitions (which map naturally onto bene's Benchmark ABC — arc_agi3 is an existence proof and is already one of the six target ladders), the score/objective schema per ladder, run attestation, and rendering frontier.json + verdict engrams. bene's compute_pareto is a pure reusable function and the /harnesses/<id>/ archive layout is a ready-made candidate-ledger schema.

The Pareto measurement engine (MVP) can be bootstrapped from bene primitives: SearchConfig.objectives syntax ('+accuracy','-context_cost'), compute_pareto, and the archive format — agentdex's job is the adapter layer that turns 'a user's self-evolving agent + a chosen ladder' into Benchmark.score() calls, plus fixing two gaps: benchmark registry hardcoding (needs a plugin/entry-point mechanism) and per-problem timeout division (too tight for SWE-Bench-Pro-style runs).

Promotion integrity is a differentiator vs Kaggle-style raw leaderboards: reuse bene's contract verbatim — pre-registered sha256-locked gates, admissibility self-test (VOID a gate that can't kill the baseline), heldout-manifest overlap ⇒ VOID, ACCEPT-only promotion, and L4-is-human-only autonomy. An agentdex leaderboard entry backed by a verdict engram chain is auditable end-to-end from one bene.db file.

BYO-creds requirement is already satisfied by the substrate: ClaudeCodeProvider runs `claude --print` on the user's own subscription, evaluation is local, and the entire run (code, traces, frontier, verdicts) is one portable SQLite file — agentdex the coordinator only needs to ingest frontier.json + promotion.json + verdict engrams, never credentials.

If session-mode is the chosen weco↔mh wiring, the mh_next_iteration gap (no genome bridge, no gated auto-promote on the collaborative path) must be fixed upstream or bridged manually via genome_from_candidate — otherwise leaderboard promotion silently loses its gate on exactly the path users will run.

Security boundary: mh's exec()-in-process evaluation is fine for the user's OWN agent on their OWN machine (matches requirement 4), but any future hosted tier must add container isolation — bene explicitly disclaims OS sandboxing.
open_questions: Can the weco CLI be driven as an MCP client or wrapped so each weco step (or run) lands in mh_submit_candidate? Zero evidence in bene-main; must come from the weco lane (weco docs seed / aideml).

Which claim is canonical on the mh→kill-gate loop: SKILL.md 2026-06-16 ('NOT wired, CLAIMS-AUDIT: partial') vs working-tree code (autopromote.py wired, opt-in, default off)? CLAIMS-AUDIT.md is gone from the tree (branch fix/landing-delink-claims-audit suggests deliberate delinking); needs git history or maintainer confirmation.

Paper claims (6x harness spread, 15+pt trace ablation, CORAL 3-10x coevolution gains, compaction quality table) are cited only from bene docs; arXiv:2603.28052 / 2604.01658 not independently verified (no web fetch in this lane).

Can Benchmark.score() cleanly express whole-agent objectives like wall-clock latency and $ cost? Score dicts are free-form floats so probably, but no shipped benchmark does it (arc_agi3's -actions is the closest); needs a spike.

How does a long agentic run (minutes-to-hours per problem, SWE-Bench Pro / TerminalBench2) interact with evaluator's asyncio.wait_for(timeout_seconds/len(problems)) and max_parallel_evals in practice? Config allows raising it, but no evidence of runs at that scale in-tree.
bene.db WAL concurrency limits under agentdex-scale parallel searches (per-thread connections, 30s busy_timeout) — architecture.md describes the mechanism but no load measurements were found.

Does mh_resume work for collaborative-mode searches (state keys pending_candidates/collaborative) or only autonomous ones? The resume handler requires benchmark match and rebuilds from archive; collaborative resume path untested in anything I read.
